name: I’ve been burned by SEO agencies before. How should I approach voice search SEO differently this time?

mainEntity.name: I’ve been burned by SEO agencies before. How should I approach voice search SEO differently this time?

acceptedAnswer.text:
If you’ve had a bad SEO experience, treat voice search SEO as a focused, measurable project rather than a vague retainer.

Define specific voice-style queries and pages upfront so you can clearly see whether this round of work delivers different results.

**Key factors:**
- Start with a limited scope: 3–5 pages and your main local profiles.
- Require clear deliverables: updated content, schemas, and performance metrics.
- Ask for baseline and follow-up data on question-based queries.
- Avoid long-term contracts until you’ve seen tangible improvements.

Use your past experience as a checklist: identify what went wrong (no reporting, generic tactics, unclear goals) and insist those issues be addressed in any new voice SEO engagement. Consider running a small pilot before expanding.

An honest provider will welcome a test project and transparent measurement instead of asking for blind trust.
acceptedAnswer.description: Approach voice search SEO as a tightly scoped, measurable project. Focus on a few high-value pages and local profiles, insist on specific deliverables (content, schema, speed fixes), and require before-and-after reporting on voice-style queries. Avoid long contracts until you see clear improvements from a pilot engagement.
